article_title: Pudu Robotics Unveils the D7, a Semi-Humanoid Industrial Robot Built on Its PuduFM Foundation Model
summary: Accurate, neutral report on Pudu's D7 launch; every specific verified against snapshots. The lone finding is three off-allowlist trade outlets, but all claims also trace to the on-allowlist PRNewswire release — an editorial warning, not a correctable factual error.

content_preview.title: Pudu Robotics Unveils the D7, a Semi-Humanoid Industrial Robot Built on Its PuduFM Foundation Model
content_preview.summary: Pudu Robotics launched the D7, a dual-arm semi-humanoid industrial robot running its PuduFM 1.0 foundation model, with a 14 kg payload and 2-meter reach for warehouse work.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Pudu Robotics, the Chinese company best known for its restaurant and hospitality service robots, has moved into heavy industrial automation with the launch of the PUDU D7, which it describes as a next-generation industrial semi-humanoid robot designed for manufacturing and industrial environments, according to [The AI Insider](https://theaiinsider.tech/2026/06/03/chinas-pudu-robotics-unveils-industrial-sem-humanoid-robot-pudu-d7/). The company [announced the D7 on June 1, 2026](http...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Clean, well-structured News piece (483 words, within the News 400-1200 range). Clear section scaffolding (Overview / What We Know / Company Context / What We Don't Know), appropriate technical depth, and a candid 'What We Don't Know' section that flags the absence of price, named customers, deployment volumes, and third-party benchmarks. Every specific is attributed inline to a named outlet.
editor_notes.source_verification: All four source snapshots fetched at HTTP 200 and read from disk (no re-WebFetch). source-0.html.gz (PRNewswire, ON allowlist) — CONFIRMED: June 1, 2026 SHENZHEN dateline, 'over 130,000 units globally' in 'more than 85 countries and regions', the sector list, the 'Born Intelligent. Transforming Industries. Reimagining Life.' tagline, PuduAgent, dual front-and-rear LiDAR / 360°, autonomous battery swapping / 24/7, and 14 kg (30.9 lbs) / 2 m (6.6 ft) specs. source-1.html.gz (Robotics Business News) — CONFIRMED: PuduFM 1.0 as embodied intelligence foundation model, the verbatim 'industrial-grade evolvable semi-humanoid robot—born from real-world scenarios and evolved through foundation models' quote, LiDAR/360°, millimeter-level force-control, battery swapping/24-7, tagline. source-2.html.gz (The AI Insider) — CONFIRMED: 14 kg / 2 m specs, the 'integrates scenario-driven intelligence, large-model reasoning, and practical business value' quote, 130,000 units / 85 countries, June 3 2026 byline. source-3.html.gz (RoboticsTomorrow) — CONFIRMED: June 1 2026 announcement / dateline, PuduAgent embodied agent architecture, sector list. No fabricated or misattributed specifics found; every direct quote appears verbatim in at least one snapshot.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All numbers (14 kg / 30.9 lbs, 2 m / 6.6 ft, 130,000 units, 85 countries), dates (June 1, 2026 launch), quotes, and product features trace to the snapshots. The article's sector list ('retail, hospitality, manufacturing, real estate and property services, healthcare, education, and public services') is a faithful subset of the source list, which additionally names 'entertainment and sport' — an omission, not a fabrication; nothing was invented. The Tesla/Figure AI framing in the Overview and 'What We Don't Know' is unattributed market context (competitors in the humanoid-robot space), which is legitimate editorial contextualization rather than a sourced factual claim, and is not overstated.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Marketing language from Pudu is consistently framed as the company's own claims ('Pudu says', 'the company markets', 'advertises'), not asserted as fact. No sensationalism, no AI self-reference.
editor_notes.originality: Original. No prior Pudu coverage in the archive (grep for 'pudu' across src/content/articles returned nothing). Adjacent June 2026 robotics coverage exists (Cainiao's Zeebot rack-climbing warehouse robot, Vingroup's humanoid entry, RoboEra, UBTech) but none covers the D7 or overlaps this story's substance.
concerns: Three of the four sources (roboticsbusinessnews.com, theaiinsider.tech, roboticstomorrow.com) are not on config/source_allowlist.txt. They are legitimate robotics/AI trade publications that republish the same PRNewswire press release. Because every claim they support is also present in the on-allowlist PRNewswire snapshot, this is a source-provenance warning rather than a factual defect. Judged editorially acceptable; the allowlist was not modified (out of scope for reviews).
recommendations: If these three outlets recur in future submissions, a maintainer may consider evaluating them for the allowlist separately from the review process.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE. Accurate, neutral, well-sourced, and original. The single automated warning (off-allowlist trade outlets) is not a correctable factual error — there is nothing to inform readers of via a corrections note, since the core claims all trace to the on-allowlist PRNewswire wire release. No corrections record is warranted.
